title: Declassified Summary: High-Altitude Weather Balloon Program
text: This synthetic, original summary describes a fictional public-records release about a high-altitude weather balloon research program. Balloons carried instruments to measure pressure, temperature, and cosmic radiation in the upper atmosphere. The program documented launch schedules, recovery procedures, and data calibration methods. Records emphasize that the balloons were scientific instruments. The summary is provided as an example document for indexing and contains no real classified material.
